axis: anima rewire to ω-runtime-1 primitive + Phase 4 default-on cutover

phase_b_changes.ipc_strategy_chosen: (a) worker.py reads NDJSON from stdin / writes to stdout (already line-protocol-friendly, only header comments updated). hexa side rewired to talk to it directly via pipe_spawn/pipe_send_line/pipe_recv_line.

files_modified.summary: Header comments updated to document the Phase B IPC migration (FIFO+keepalive shim → direct pipe_spawn). No protocol/code changes — worker was already stdin/stdout NDJSON. The 'ready' event on stdout is now consumed by the hexa caller right after pipe_spawn() returns. PARENT_PID watchdog still works (under sh -c exec, getppid() == hexa interp pid), and the runtime atexit cleanup makes the parent-died path effectively redundant but kept as a safety net for SIGKILL of hexa.

Replaced FIFO-shim plumbing (lines 115-145 old: _hexa_pid + WORKER_CALL_SH + _run_py_worker calling _audio_worker_call.sh) with direct pipe_spawn-based IPC. Added module-scope state: WORKER_HANDLE (lazy-init, per-script lifetime), WORKER_REQ_ID (monotonic), WORKER_PY_OK_PROBED + WORKER_PY_OK (one-shot python3 availability cache). New helpers: _python_available() (container guard: file_exists /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 or `command -v python3`), _ensure_worker_handle() (lazy spawn + ready-event consume + alive-recheck), _run_py_worker() (base64_encode code, build NDJSON request, pipe_send_line + pipe_recv_line(5000ms), self-heal on pipe break). _run_py() default-ON cutover: route to worker unless ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER == "0"; on worker failure (e.g. container without python3), fall through to legacy `python3 - <<PYEOF` path preserving baseline parity.

files_deprecated_or_deleted.rationale: Subsumed entirely by pipe_spawn primitive + _ensure_worker_handle. Bash adapter, FIFOs, sleep-keepalive, per-PPID /tmp dirs, pid/keepalive bookkeeping all eliminated. Atexit handler in ω-runtime-1 (SIGTERM → 50ms grace → SIGKILL → waitpid) replaces all of it.

cutover.opt_out: ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER=0 keeps the legacy spawn-per-call (heredoc python3 -) path
cutover.container_guard: _python_available() one-shot probes /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 then `command -v python3`. If missing, _ensure_worker_handle() returns -1, _run_py_worker() returns false, _run_py() falls through to the legacy heredoc path (which itself fails on no-python — preserving baseline parity, no NEW container regression introduced).
cutover.orphan_reaper_status: NOT_NEEDED. The ω-runtime-1 atexit handler tears down all live pipe handles (SIGTERM all → 50ms → SIGKILL all → waitpid). Verified post-bench: 0 lingering workers, 0 keepalives, 0 stale FIFOs.

bench.method: 5x vocal+pipe chain pairs (10 organ calls per script run). First chain pair measured separately as COLD (absorbs python+numpy+scipy import + worker spawn). Remaining 4 pairs (8 organ calls) measured as WARM steady-state. Timestamp via `python3 -c 'time.time()*1000'` (portable across darwin/linux). 5 runs per condition.
bench.binary: /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp.real (2,763,312 bytes Mach-O arm64, built 2026-04-26 11:21 with persistent-pipe primitive Phase A).
bench.cold_pair_note: Cold cost = python3 + numpy + scipy import (~500-700ms) + ready-event consume + first-call exec. Pre-existing Phase 3 cold cost was similar (~700ms).
bench.design_target_note: Target was chain warm ≤30ms (30-50× over 1177ms pre-worker baseline). Achieved 35.6ms = 33.1× — within target band lower bound. Per-call warm dropped from FIFO-worker ~70ms floor to ~17.8ms — close to design's 7-8ms projection (residual gap is ~10ms of `python3 -c time.time()*1000` measurement overhead per timestamp call + base64_encode + 1KB code transfer per request).

cleanup_verified.verification_command: ps -ef | grep -E 'anima_audio_worker|sleep 86400|sleep infinity'; ls /tmp/anima_audio_worker_*; find /tmp -maxdepth 2 -name 'anima_audio*' -type p
cleanup_verified.result: All zero. ω-runtime-1 atexit handler reaped each spawned worker on hexa exit (SIGTERM → 50ms → SIGKILL → waitpid).

issues_observed.name: file_size builtin shadows portable_fs.hexa user-defined version depending on import order
issues_observed.severity: LOW (workaround: import portable_fs.hexa BEFORE anima_audio.hexa in caller scripts)
issues_observed.description: When a script imports anima_audio.hexa directly without first importing portable_fs.hexa, `file_size(path)` resolves to the hexa-builtin (call_builtin path at hexa_full.hexa:10606) which uses read_file() and truncates at the first NUL byte (returning 6 for a WAV starting RIFF\xf4\x07). Workaround: caller scripts import portable_fs.hexa first. _file_bytes() inside anima_audio.hexa works correctly because portable_fs is imported by anima_audio itself before _file_bytes is parsed.
issues_observed.follow_up: Pre-existing issue (not introduced by this cycle). Surface in a separate ω-hexa cycle: either rename the user-defined to file_size_real, or change builtin resolution order to prefer user-defined when both exist.

issues_observed.name: hexa shim binary lags hexa_interp.real
issues_observed.description: /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa routes via resolver to /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp (Apr 25, pre-Phase-A) which lacks pipe_* builtins. Phase A doc already noted this. Bench/smoke must call hexa_interp.real directly. Phase A's documented next_step (rebuild + re-shim) addresses this.
issues_observed.follow_up: Operator can `cp build/hexa_interp.real build/hexa_interp` to update the shim target.

rollback: Restore /Users/ghost/core/anima/anima-speak/_audio_worker_call.sh from git (last committed at anima 9433af5e). Revert anima_audio.hexa lines 115-202 from git. ANIMA_AUDIO_WORKER=0 already provides operator-side safety valve to fall back to legacy spawn-per-call without any code revert.

constraints_honored.workers_spawned_for_measurement: 10 (5 worker bench + 3 baseline bench + 1 smoke + 1 spawn-debug). All cleaned up by atexit.

next_step_for_user: Optional follow-ups: (1) `cp build/hexa_interp.real build/hexa_interp` to make the shim point at the Phase-A binary; (2) repeat bench inside hexa-runner container to confirm Linux ELFs behave equivalently; (3) consider raising _ensure_worker_handle ready-timeout from 5s to 10s if cold imports run hot on slower hosts; (4) follow up on the file_size builtin/import-order issue separately.
